metadata.from: Bob Hanifen — signed 'Love Bob' at the end; written on University of Wyoming / Laramie letterhead with 'Cowboy Joe' mascot
topics: health / illness (D.D. or drizzly gg from cantaloupe)
metadata.mood_tone: Chatty, upbeat, and slightly humorous; somewhat homesick but energetic; apologetic about forgetting stamps and not selling the clothes yet
metadata.legibility_notes: Several words are difficult to read due to cursive style and ink bleed. 'D.D.' or 'Drizzly gg' (likely 'GI' — stomach illness) on page 2 is unclear. 'ration book' partially obscured. Some words guessed with uncertainty.
metadata.historical_context: Letter references a ration book, suggesting WWII-era rationing. Military training context (MP Barracks, inspections, P.T., Military hour) indicates Bob is in a military training program, likely ASTP or similar college-based military program at University of Wyoming during WWII.
metadata.date_estimation_reasoning: Letter is written on University of Wyoming 'Cowboy Joe' letterhead from Laramie. References a ration book (WWII rationing), military training schedule (P.T., Military hour, inspections), and a girlfriend 20 miles out with chicken dinner. Letter 16 (dated July 13, 1944) from Dad to Jim Jr. explicitly mentions Bob's ASTP address at University of Wyoming, Laramie — 'He starts off at 6 AM + goes right thru until 10:30 PM.' This matches Bob's description in Letter 7 exactly. Letter 18a (also Wyoming, multi-day letter starting Monday) references Frontier Days in Cheyenne (held annually in late July) and a September 30–October 10 leave, also consistent with 1944. Letter 7 mentions 'tomorrow we have English first hour, Math 2 hours, 1 hr. of P.T., 3 hr of Physics Lab, 1 hr. of Military' — matches ASTP curriculum. Dated July 10, which in 1944 falls on a Monday; the letter reads as written mid-week given the next-day schedule described. Estimated July 1944.

pages.transcription:
July, 10

Dear Dad

     I have wrote a couple times telling you the clothes fit & the shoes. I haven't sold them as yet because the guy I was going to sell them to broke a bone in his foot & can't play any more. I will send them back in about a week if can't sell them. Say you can my shoes stunk in my [ration?] book. If they haven't turned them in, I wait till I hear from you before I do anything. Sorry about the cars, I say they are really getting rough. If you don't pass at the [first?] 12 week term you get sent home. But they say it isn't much of a change though. I wrote Edith & John and forgot to put a stamp on the letter, I was in a pretty big hurry and I forgot the stamp. I wish I knew sooner about Russ Wellman because I though[t] was

there last week in fact we slept there at the M.P. Barracks. I write more often but I don't get a lot of time, always having inspection, room & personal, you have to do it even if you have [the?] fuss. They really are strict. Slight delay. It is now 10:15 almost time for bed. We to eat chow had ear corn, dressing, potato, milk, cookies and roast beef it was really good. We had a basketball game at 7:00 it was a pretty good game I played about 2 quarters and boy I sure am tired. Thanks for buck and the 2 buck bill. I am going out to one of my girl friend house Sun. She lives about 20 miles out. She is going to have chicken for dinner. God help her if she don't. I have had chicken only 3 times since I left home than it would make a [person?] a good brother, it was little and it sure cost me. I got the D.D. [or?] Drizzly [gg?] from eating cantaloupe and I fell sort of low, but I am getting over it now. Well tomorrow we have English first hour, Math 2 hours, 1 hr. of P.T., 3 hr of Physics Lab, 1 hr. of Military and a rigid personal inspection so I had better get to bed. This high altitude makes you sleepy all the time.

Love Bob

full_transcription:
July, 10

Dear Dad

     I have wrote a couple times telling you the clothes fit & the shoes. I haven't sold them as yet because the guy I was going to sell them to broke a bone in his foot & can't play any more. I will send them back in about a week if can't sell them. Say you can my shoes stunk in my [ration?] book. If they haven't turned them in, I wait till I hear from you before I do anything. Sorry about the cars, I say they are really getting rough. If you don't pass at the [first?] 12 week term you get sent home. But they say it isn't much of a change though. I wrote Edith & John and forgot to put a stamp on the letter, I was in a pretty big hurry and I forgot the stamp. I wish I knew sooner about Russ Wellman because I though[t] was there last week in fact we slept there at the M.P. Barracks. I write more often but I don't get a lot of time, always having inspection, room & personal, you have to do it even if you have [the?] fuss. They really are strict. Slight delay. It is now 10:15 almost time for bed. We to eat chow had ear corn, dressing, potato, milk, cookies and roast beef it was really good. We had a basketball game at 7:00 it was a pretty good game I played about 2 quarters and boy I sure am tired. Thanks for buck and the 2 buck bill. I am going out to one of my girl friend house Sun. She lives about 20 miles out. She is going to have chicken for dinner. God help her if she don't. I have had chicken only 3 times since I left home than it would make a [person?] a good brother, it was little and it sure cost me. I got the D.D. [or?] Drizzly [gg?] from eating cantaloupe and I fell sort of low, but I am getting over it now. Well tomorrow we have English first hour, Math 2 hours, 1 hr. of P.T., 3 hr of Physics Lab, 1 hr. of Military and a rigid personal inspection so I had better get to bed. This high altitude makes you sleepy all the time.

Love Bob
summary: Bob writes to his father from the University of Wyoming in Laramie, where he is in a military training program. He updates his dad on his attempts to sell clothes and shoes, mentions forgetting to stamp a letter to Edith and John, and references knowing Russ Wellman and staying at the M.P. Barracks. He describes a busy military-academic schedule including English, Math, Physics Lab, P.T., and Military classes, notes he got a stomach illness from cantaloupe, played basketball, and is planning a Sunday visit to a girlfriend who lives 20 miles out and promises chicken dinner.
